commit.message:
feat: intelligent reminder lead time based on task type

- Meeting/appointment: remind 30 min before (not at start time)
- Payment/bills: remind 3 days before
- Deadline/submission: remind 1 day before
- Travel/flight: remind 2 hours before
- Medical appointment: remind 1 hour before
- Other timed tasks: remind 15 min before
- No time specified: remind at 9am on due date

Also: reminder confirmation always shows actual remind time vs due time

commit.message:
fix: critical call connection issues — bidirectional video + error handling

Call Page Fixes:
- Remove cyrano from handleLobbyJoin dependency array (was causing instability)
- Use ref pattern for cyrano activation (cyranoEnabledOnJoinRef)
- Separate side effect into useEffect for proper lifecycle

PeerConnection Fixes:
- Add bidirectional video call initiation (both host AND guest now call)
- Host calls guest after 1.5s delay as backup
- Add 8-second stream timeout with automatic retry
- Add exponential backoff retry (2s → 4s → 8s, max 3 attempts)
- Add comprehensive error handling for all failure modes:
  - peer.call() returns null
  - No stream within timeout
  - call.on('error')
  - call.on('close') before stream
  - ICE connection failure
  - RTCPeerConnection failure
- Status updates to "reconnecting" during retry attempts
- Proper cleanup of all timeouts on disconnect

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add DCVC_Test: DCVC-RT vs v5 evaluation on v2_resized

- run_dcvc.py: encode/decode 65-frame sequence with DCVC-RT (CVPR 2025)
  at 4 QP points; fixed 3 bugs (missing YCbCr conversion, wrong decode QP,
  missing C++ ANS extension path)

  removed erroneous /3 BPP division, added combined comparison plot
- dcvc_psnr_bpp.png: DCVC-RT R-D curve (28.4–37.9 dB, 0.003–0.142 BPP)
- psnr_bpp_comparison.png: v5 vs DCVC-RT overlay; DCVC-RT leads by 4–7 dB
- steps.txt: step-by-step procedure including all bugs diagnosed and fixed
- summary.txt: results table, observations, and file index
- DCVC_Test/CLAUDE.md: full session notes for Claude Code continuity

Bump devDependencies to latest versions (#300)

* Bump devDependencies to latest versions

- jest: ^29.5.0 → ^29.7.0
- ts-jest: ^29.1.1 → ^29.4.6
- @types/jest: ^29.5.2 → ^29.5.14
- @types/node: ^20.3.3 → ^24.0.0 (aligns with Node 24 target)
- typescript: ^5.1.6 → ^5.9.3
- kelonio: ^0.9.0 → ^0.11.0
- @stryker-mutator/*: ^7.1.0 → ^9.6.0
- husky: ^8.0.3 → ^9.1.7

Update husky prepare script and pre-push hook for v9 syntax.
All tests pass; mutation score remains 100%.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Unroll for loops in generateCusip to eliminate timeout mutations

Stryker's UpdateOperator mutator changed `i++` to `i--` in both
for loops, creating infinite loops that could only be killed via
timeout. Unrolling the loops removes the mutation targets entirely.

Mutation timeouts: 2 → 0, run time: ~84s → ~41s.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

split two-column layout. Add click-outside-to-close behavior on

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix paste reliability, instance lock, debounce, and security hardening

Bug fixes:
- Paste no longer fails silently: retries on cooldown, reports errors,
  preserves clipboard contents, shows transcribed text even if paste fails
- Instance lock handles PID recycling after crash (checks process name)
- Reduced PTT debounce from 300ms to 150ms to stop blocking quick re-records
- Transcription thread waits instead of silently dropping when busy
- Done hint now shows actual PTT key instead of hardcoded "fn"
- Overlay state file written atomically to prevent corrupt JSON reads

Security fixes:
- Removed hardcoded API key from install.sh and install.ps1
- Added .env to .gitignore to prevent key leaks
- Replaced shell-injection-vulnerable env loading (export $(grep|xargs))
  with safe _safe_load_env() that validates key names
- Lock and state files created with 0600 permissions

commit.message:
Optimización velocidad: lightbox preload ±1 (no todas), lazy thumbnails, timeout en batch fetch, dedup syncToday, eliminar groupDatesByMonth duplicado + mensaje del desarrollador en settings + v4.1 + SW v107

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Major upgrade: Memory, Task Queue, ReAct, Heartbeat, Inter-Agent Chat

5 OpenClaw-inspired improvements:

1. AGENT MEMORY — Persistent markdown memory per agent. After each task,
   Claude extracts learnings and saves them. Memory is injected into
   future tasks so agents get smarter over time. Auto-compacts at 10KB.

2. TASK QUEUE — Per-agent serial queue (OpenClaw Lane Queue pattern).
   When a task finishes, the next 'todo' task auto-runs. Priority
   ordering (critical > high > medium > low). No more manual "Run" clicks.

3. ReAct LOOP — Multi-step reasoning (Reason → Act → Observe → Repeat).
   Agents can do up to 3 reasoning steps per task. Can consult other
   agents mid-task via [CONSULT:agent_id] syntax.

4. HEARTBEAT — Scheduled proactive tasks:
   - Auto-standup every 8 hours
   - Queue monitor every 60 seconds (finds idle agents with pending tasks)
   - Memory compaction every 24 hours

5. INTER-AGENT CHAT — Agents can ask each other questions mid-task.
   Consultations appear in team chat. Memory from consulted agent is
   included for context-aware responses.

New API endpoints:
- GET /api/agents/:id/memory — Read agent memory
- DELETE /api/agents/:id/memory — Clear agent memory
- POST /api/agents/:fromId/consult/:toId — Inter-agent consultation
- GET /api/heartbeat — Heartbeat job status + queue status

feat(dashboard): Issue #3 — Dashboard Lite (shopping-only interface)

Add docs/dashboard-lite.html — a self-contained React SPA that exposes
only the Shopping List, intended as a shared household interface for
Victoria (and Ahmed) without VERA's full intelligence layer.

Features (identical to main dashboard shopping tab):
- Store sub-tabs with pending item count badges
- Toggle items done/undone (optimistic update + revert on error)
- Add Item modal with store selector and keyboard shortcuts
- Settings modal (⚙) for first-time URL + token setup
- Refresh button (↺) for manual reload
- Error banner with dismiss + toast notifications
- Full mobile responsive layout

Architecture:
- ~580 lines vs 3,300+ in index.html
- Shares vera_url / vera_token localStorage keys — users already
  connected on main dashboard need no re-entry on same origin
- No backend changes — uses existing shopping / shopping_toggle /
  shopping_add Web App actions
- ShoppingTab, AddItemModal, SettingsModal copied verbatim from
  index.html so behaviour is pixel-identical

GitHub Pages URL: /dashboard-lite.html (same domain as index.html)

Closes #3

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add auto-troubleshoot and retry for failed tasks

When a task fails:
1. Troubleshooter diagnoses WHY it failed (error + logs analysis)
2. Posts diagnosis to chat (problem, fix, retryable?)
3. If retryable: updates task description with fix, resets to 'todo', auto-reruns after 3s
4. If not retryable: marks as needs manual intervention
5. Max 2 retries before giving up
6. Skips troubleshooting if user manually stopped the task

commit.message:
Reduce concurrency and add retry logic for large synced MP3 builds

Signed URL batches reduced from 20 to 5, download batches from 10 to 5,
with back-pressure pauses and 3 retries with backoff to prevent HTTP/2
connection pool exhaustion on large recordings (840+ clips).

- Language toggle: padding 0.5rem → 0.75rem (touch target 28px → 44px+)
- Language toggle font: 0.8rem → 0.875rem (14px)
- Subtitle font: 0.82rem → 0.875rem (14px, above 11px absolute minimum)
- "from the" label: 0.68rem → 0.75rem (12px, was 10.9px — below minimum)
- Verse reference font: 0.8rem → 0.875rem + wordBreak: break-word
- Verse text: added wordBreak: break-word (prevents horizontal overflow)
- Download button font: 0.82rem → 0.875rem + className btn-download
- Download button: removed onMouseEnter/Leave (not triggered on touch)
- Footer font: 0.72rem → 0.75rem (12px)

fix: show every document in Clio import progress (not every 5th)

Communications and notes import progress was only updating every 5th
item (idx % 5 == 0), causing the counter to skip from e.g. "5 of 65"
to "10 of 65". Documents already updated every item. Now all three
sections report progress for every document processed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Consolidate source files and improve mutation test coverage (#298)

Reduced 21 files (12 source + 9 test) down to 6 (4 source + 3 test) by
collapsing single-line trivial wrappers into their only call sites.

Source changes:
- Add src/constants.ts — replaces 3 fixture files (alphabet, base-ten-numbers,
  alphanumeric-values) with a single ALPHABET/BASE_TEN/ALPHANUMERIC export
- Rewrite src/generate-cusip.ts — inlines generateCusipWithoutCheckCode and
  all random-string helpers into a single 5-line function using a local
  randomFrom<T> utility; fixes the latent bug where package.json pointed at
  build/index.js but no src/index.ts existed
- Simplify src/generate-cusip-check-code.ts — inlines isOdd (i % 2 === 1)
  and convertStringToBaseTenNumber (parseInt) directly
- Add src/index.ts — public re-export that makes build/index.js resolvable

Test changes:
- Delete 7 spec files that tested only inlined one-liners
- Add src/__tests__/constants.spec.ts — exact toEqual assertions on
  ALPHABET, BASE_TEN and ALPHANUMERIC; kills all 24 surviving StringLiteral
  and ArrayDeclaration mutants in constants.ts
- Add check-code test case "037831AG" → 9; kills the two surviving
  ArithmeticOperator mutants (sum of v_i%10 = 26, not divisible by 5 or 10,
  so + vs - and %10 vs *10 diverge)
- Add generate-cusip test with Math.random mocked to 0.9; asserts output
  "999999667", killing the Math.random() / arr.length mutant

Mutation score: 57.81% → 100% (62 killed, 0 survived, 2 timeout)

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add Sentinel auto-QA review for all completed tasks

After any agent finishes a task:
1. Sentinel reviews the output (correctness, completeness, security, quality)
2. Posts a scored review (X/10, PASS/NEEDS WORK/FAIL) to team chat
3. If FAIL or score ≤4, auto-creates a high-priority fix task for the original agent
4. Review details saved to task logs
5. Then generates follow-up tasks as before

Sentinel skips reviewing its own work to prevent infinite loops.

commit.message:
feat: NL-to-graph training pairs from 12 verified public schemas

Fuzzy tier — messy natural language input mapped to production-ready
graph schemas. Ground truth from Neo4j example datasets (Movies,
Northwind, POLE, StackOverflow, Healthcare, FinCEN, Network Management,
Recommendations), ICIJ Offshore Leaks, and OGB benchmarks (MAG, BioKG).

43 pairs across 9 industries, 46 node labels, 52 relationship types.
3 prompt quality levels (clean/casual/messy) per schema.
Validator-clean: 0 errors, 0 convention violations.

commit.message:
Add master level system: goldsmith workshop, farm station, new materials, and wax seal progression

Phase 1 of the master level based on Brunelleschi's goldsmith apprenticeship research:
- 8 new materials: sulfur, copper, gold, herbs, letame, charred ox horn, beeswax, eggs
- 8 new crafted items: raw bronze, gold leaf, fumigation incense, casting mold, tempera paint, 3 wax seals
- Farm station (La Fattoria) with pitchfork tool for collecting animal-based materials
- Goldsmith workshop (Bottega di Benincasa Lotti) as new SpriteKit interior scene
- WorkshopView supports 3 interiors: outdoor, crafting room, goldsmith
- Wax seal progression system: apprentice (beeswax+clay), architect (beeswax+copper), master (beeswax+gold)
- Updated bronze recipes to use historically accurate copper proportions
